summary: Epic Days Between centerpiece with strong Stagger Lee and Broken Arrow
blurb: The show shines with a transcendent Days Between and a tight Stagger Lee early in the first set. Highlights include Broken Arrow’s bass work, a fiery New Speedway > Nobody's Fault sequence, and an emotional Miracle followed by Lovelight. The performance is strong overall despite occasional pitch drift and a meandering drum/space segment.
review: On December 19, 1994 at the Sports Arena in Los Angeles, the Dead delivered a set that still feels fresh for late‑era fans. The first set opened with Picasso Moon before settling into a solid groove anchored by Stagger Lee and Broken Arrow – the latter drawing particular praise for Phil’s bass line. Jerry’s voice was “full of emotion” on Days Between, where he nailed the lyrics and delivered a heartfelt solo that many reviewers called “achingly nostalgic.” The second set built momentum with Saint Of Circumstance and a standout New Speedway Boogie > Nobody's Fault But Mine segue, followed by an extended ten‑minute drum jam that led into a soaring I Need A Miracle. Days Between rose to the show’s summit, then gently faded into Turn On Your Lovelight and a tender Brokedown Palace encore. While the soundboard source suffers occasional pitch drift and the Drum/Space segment was called tedious by some listeners, the overall musicality – especially Jerry’s emotive vocals, Phil’s bass on Broken Arrow, Bob’s inspired work on Miracle and Lovelight, and the tight rhythm section – makes this concert a must‑listen for anyone tracking the band’s 1994 run.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings describe many high points – Days Between, Stagger Lee, Broken Arrow, New Speedway > Nobody's Fault, Miracle and Lovelight – with enthusiastic language (e.g., "transcendent", "stellar", "emotive vocals"). Negative remarks are limited to technical pitch drift and a tedious Drum/Space segment. The overall tone across the two sources is more positive than negative, warranting a moderate +0.8 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source captures the full set with crisp board detail; despite occasional pitch drift it includes all standout moments and is the most complete recording.

key_highlights: Transcendent Days Between that anchors the second set.
Tight Stagger Lee early in Set 1 and a strong Broken Arrow bass showcase.
Fiery New Speedway Boogie > Nobody's Fault But Mine sequence.
Emotive Miracle leading into Lovelight, ending with a heartfelt Brokedown Palace encore.

band_performance.Jerry: Emotive vocals, especially on Days Between and Saint Of Circumstance; solid solo on New Speedway Boogie despite a few off‑pitch moments early in So Many Roads.
band_performance.Phil: Bass shines on Broken Arrow; his playing underpins the nostalgic feel of Days Between as noted by Lesh’s quote.
band_performance.Bob: Strong contributions on Miracle and Lovelight, with several reviewers calling his playing among the best in years.
band_performance.Mickey: Provides a solid rhythmic foundation; part of the extended ~10‑minute jam before Miracle but the Drum/Space segment was viewed as tedious by some listeners.
band_performance.Billy: Works tightly with Mickey throughout, delivering a dependable groove and supporting the long drum/space improvisation.
